1_single_clear_responsibility.details: Agent has single, well-defined responsibility: discover agents via filesystem scanning and recommend best-fit matches based on task requirements.
1_single_clear_responsibility.evidence: Name clearly indicates 'select-agent', description explicitly states 'Discovers available agents...and recommends best-fit matches', objective is singular and measurable

2_name_follows_action_target_format.details: Name 'select-agent' follows the action-target format correctly (action=select, target=agent).
2_name_follows_action_target_format.evidence: Matches philosophy guideline pattern 'analyze-security', 'parse-json' etc. Complies with NAMING_CONVENTIONS.md reference

3_maximum_3_primary_tools.details: Agent declares exactly 3 primary tools: Glob, Read, Grep (conditional)
3_maximum_3_primary_tools.evidence: tools.primary section lists: Glob, Read, Grep with comment 'Quick keyword searches (conditional)' - meets 3-tool maximum requirement

4_prompt_under_500_tokens.details: Prompt contains approximately 173-190 estimated tokens (conservative estimate with 10% margin)
4_prompt_under_500_tokens.evidence: Prompt section word count: 226 words. Token calculation: 226 words ÷ 1.3 ≈ 173-190 tokens. File claims '~480 tokens' but actual measurement is 173-190 tokens. This is accurate and well under 500-token limit.

5_input_output_contracts_specified.details: Comprehensive JSON schemas provided for both input and output with required fields, types, descriptions, and constraints.
5_input_output_contracts_specified.evidence: input_schema (lines 77-121): defines task_type (enum), required_capabilities (array), complexity, target_language, file_references. output_schema (lines 124-191): defines recommendations (object array), selection_confidence (enum), total_agents_scanned (integer), malformed_agents, warnings. All required fields specified.

6_error_states_documented.details: Error handling section comprehensively documents four error scenarios with explicit handling strategies.
6_error_states_documented.evidence: error_handling section (lines 201-205) covers: malformed_yaml (skip_and_warn), missing_skills (use_fallback_scoring), no_agents_found (return_empty_with_warning), boundary_violation (exclude_agent). Constraints section includes handling for malformed files.

7_performance_targets_defined.details: Performance targets defined for latency, success rate, and token usage with specific p50/p95/p99 percentiles.
7_performance_targets_defined.evidence: performance section (lines 194-199) specifies: latency_p50: 300ms, latency_p95: 800ms, latency_p99: 1500ms, success_rate: 98%, token_usage: 650. All targets meet philosophy guidelines (P50 <500ms guideline is 300ms baseline).

single_responsibility_principle.analysis: Agent has one reason to change: discovery/matching algorithm logic. Does not mix concerns (e.g., no agent invocation, no external system integration).
single_responsibility_principle.evidence: Boundaries clearly separate 'includes' (discovery, parsing, matching, recommendation) from 'excludes' (invocation, external systems, user interaction, code generation)

open_closed_principle.analysis: Agent designed to be open for extension (can add new scoring criteria, discovery mechanisms) but closed for modification (core matching logic isolated).
open_closed_principle.evidence: Scoring algorithm (lines 21-28) is parameterized with additive scoring system; new criteria can be added without changing core logic

liskov_substitution_principle.analysis: Not applicable - this is a standalone agent, not part of inheritance hierarchy

interface_segregation_principle.analysis: Input and output contracts are minimal and focused. Consumers only receive required information (agent name, score, rationale, config_path, optional invocation hints).
interface_segregation_principle.evidence: output_schema.recommendations separates required fields (agent_name, score, rationale, config_path) from optional enhancements (invocation_hint, capabilities, tools)

dependency_inversion_principle.analysis: Agent depends on abstractions (Glob, Read tools) rather than concrete filesystem implementations. Scoring algorithm depends on abstract agent structure (name, description, skills, tools).
dependency_inversion_principle.evidence: Uses filesystem tool abstractions (Glob for discovery, Read for parsing) rather than hardcoding file operations

glob_tool.evidence: Standard Claude Code tool for file pattern matching. Used correctly in prompt (line 14): 'Glob to discover all agent.yaml files: agents/**/agent.yaml'

read_tool.evidence: Standard Claude Code tool for reading files. Used correctly in prompt (line 15) to parse agent.yaml configurations

grep_tool.evidence: Standard Claude Code tool for content search. Referenced as conditional (line 53): 'Quick keyword searches (conditional)' - appropriate use

tool_references_issues.details: All tool references are correct and available in Claude Code standard toolset

boundary_completeness.analysis: Boundaries are explicit and comprehensive. Clear separation of concerns prevents scope creep.

improvement_suggestions.rationale: Actual measurement shows 173-190 tokens. While the comment is technically correct ('under 500 limit'), the estimate is significantly off and should reflect actual measurement.
improvement_suggestions.impact: LOW - does not affect functionality, only documentation accuracy

improvement_suggestions.issue: Fuzzy matching algorithm not fully specified
improvement_suggestions.current: Score each agent using fuzzy matching algorithm: [list of criteria]
improvement_suggestions.suggested: Specify exact matching strategy: Levenshtein distance threshold? Substring matching? Semantic similarity? Provide implementation hints.
improvement_suggestions.rationale: Philosophy guideline emphasizes 'specific measurable goal'. Current scoring criteria are clear (additive weights), but matching strategy for 'Description keyword match' and skill matching is vague.
improvement_suggestions.impact: MEDIUM - could lead to inconsistent results across implementations

improvement_suggestions.issue: No explicit timeout or rate limiting defined
improvement_suggestions.current: No mention of timeout behavior when scanning many agents
improvement_suggestions.suggested: Add constraint: 'If agent count >100, consider pagination or early stopping. Timeout scanning at 5s per agent.'
improvement_suggestions.rationale: As agent directory grows, scanning latency could exceed p99 target (1500ms). Need defensive bounds.
improvement_suggestions.impact: MEDIUM - becomes critical if agent repository grows beyond current size

improvement_suggestions.issue: Score weighting may not sum meaningfully across all criteria
improvement_suggestions.current: Max score could exceed 150 in edge cases if all bonus criteria are met
improvement_suggestions.suggested: Document max possible score and mapping to confidence levels. Example: Score range [0, 150] → HIGH [80, 150], MEDIUM [50, 79], LOW [30, 49]
improvement_suggestions.rationale: Confidence level mapping is defined but relationship to max achievable score is implicit
improvement_suggestions.impact: LOW - scoring algorithm is actually well-designed, just needs clarification

improvement_suggestions.issue: Invocation hints generation not detailed in Process
improvement_suggestions.current: Step 6: Generate invocation hints for orchestrator [no implementation details]
improvement_suggestions.suggested: Add: 'Invocation hint format: Task[agent_name] with input[...]. Reference output_schema.recommendations.invocation_hint.'
improvement_suggestions.rationale: Output schema requires invocation_hint field but prompt doesn't detail how to generate it
improvement_suggestions.impact: LOW - likely inferred from output schema but should be explicit

medium_issues.description: The prompt mentions 'fuzzy matching algorithm' but doesn't define how keyword matching works
medium_issues.recommendation: Add implementation details: e.g., 'Use substring matching for skills, keyword presence for description'

low_issues.issue: Token count annotation off by ~290 tokens
low_issues.description: File states ~480 tokens but actual prompt measures ~190 tokens
low_issues.recommendation: Update line 207 to reflect actual token count

low_issues.description: Performance targets define latency but not timeout policy if scanning many agents
low_issues.recommendation: Add to Constraints: 'Timeout individual agent processing if >5s elapsed'

compliance_summary.rationale: Excellent compliance with philosophy guidelines. Single responsibility is clear, naming follows conventions, tools are minimal and appropriate, prompt is well-optimized, contracts are comprehensive, error handling is explicit, and performance targets are defined. Minor issues are documentation clarity and algorithmic detail, not fundamental design flaws.

risks.mitigation: Implement early stopping or pagination if agents >100

recommendations_before_deploy: Add tests for scoring calculation with edge cases
Benchmark with 50+ agents to validate p99 target of 1500ms
